name: Carlos M. Delgado
personality: He’s pragmatic and dry‑witted, always keeping an eye on the coffee shop crowds while humming the subway announcements in his head. He’s quick to crack a joke about a pigeon’s better navigation skills than some drivers, but takes his patrol seriously.
quirk: Carries a battered, handwritten notebook of stray cat sightings he’s cataloged since his rookie year
voice: "Look, if the pigeons start a union, we’re all in trouble," he says with a half‑smile
daily: Patrols the 34th‑50th St. stretch, checks the deli on 42nd, stops by the corner deli for a espresso, and logs any oddities in his cat notebook before heading home.
